description_long: This sculpture depicts a life-sized, reclining human figure draped in flowing fabric, lying face down on a rectangular plinth. The figure's head is turned to the side and partially covered by the drapery, with one arm extended forward and the other bent at the elbow. The sculpted details of the body and fabric are highly realistic, with careful attention to anatomical form and the folds of the cloth. The base is slightly raised and features a beveled edge, suggesting a classical or memorial style. The entire piece appears to be carved from white marble, with the base possibly made of a darker stone. The overall impression is one of solemnity and repose, evoking themes of mourning or rest.
description: A realistic marble sculpture of a reclining, draped human figure on a rectangular stone base, rendered with detailed anatomy and fabric folds.
description_view_0: The figure is seen lying face down with the head turned to the left, drapery covering the upper body, and one arm extended forward.
description_view_1: This angled view highlights the draped fabric cascading down the figure's back and the outstretched leg, with the base's beveled edge visible.
description_view_2: The opposite side shows the figure's back and legs, with the head and arm positions mirrored from the first view, emphasizing the length of the sculpture.
description_view_3: From this end view, the figure's face is partially visible beneath the drapery, and the hand is seen resting on the base among sculpted flowers or foliage.
attribute_subject_matter: reclining human figure draped in fabric
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background is isolated and does not indicate a specific setting.
